symbols: dove descending — the Holy Spirit overshadowing Mary at the moment of the Incarnation
angel with wings — Gabriel, the messenger of God announcing the divine conception
Latin inscription AVE MARIA GRATIA PLENA DOMINUS TECUM — the angelic salutation: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee
red and gold vestments — the regal dignity of the moment of the Incarnation
banner with MARIA — identifying the recipient of the divine message
description: A stained glass window in a Gothic-pointed arch shape. At the top, a white dove representing the Holy Spirit descends with outstretched wings, radiating white light. Below, an angel with blue and white wings extends a hand. The Virgin Mary is depicted in rich red and gold vestments, her hands raised. A banner reading MARIA crosses the composition. At the bottom, a Latin inscription reads GRATIA PLENA DOMINUS TECUM. The glass uses vibrant reds, golds, blues, and whites with lead came creating the structure. Small decorative elements including flowers fill the borders.
theological_description: The Annunciation is the pivotal moment in salvation history when heaven awaited the response of a young woman. Gabriel's greeting — Ave Maria, gratia plena, Dominus tecum (Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee) — announces the beginning of the New Covenant. The descent of the Holy Spirit as a dove echoes the creation narrative where the Spirit hovered over the waters (GEN.1.2); now the Spirit hovers over Mary to bring about the new creation. The stained glass medium is itself theologically apt: as light passes through glass without breaking it, so the divine entered Mary without violating her virginity — an analogy used by medieval theologians. The Latin text inscribed at the base ensures that every worshipper who enters the church is greeted with the same words that changed history: 'The Lord is with you' (LUK.1.28). Mary's posture of receptivity embodies the faith that made the Incarnation possible: 'Let it be to me according to your word' (LUK.1.38).

scripture_refs.reason: The Annunciation — Gabriel's message to Mary and her acceptance of God's plan
The angelic greeting inscribed on the window: Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with you
The Holy Spirit will come upon you — the dove descending in the window
The virgin shall conceive and bear a son — the prophetic fulfillment announced by Gabriel
